change_log_latest:
• ⚡ Added a resilient transcript follow fallback so the bottom sentinel uses `scrollIntoView` in real browsers and automatically falls back to direct bottom pinning where that DOM API is unavailable, keeping both the shipped panel and the test harness stable.
• 🐞 Fixed the browser-smoke follow verification path so the regenerated `web-ui.test.html` no longer throws on environments like jsdom while still validating that live streaming stays attached near the bottom.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the installer/test artifacts again and rechecked the chat follow behavior directly in Playwright against the regenerated test HTML DOM.
change_log_release:
v0.8.27

• ⚡ Reworked transcript follow mode again around a persistent bottom sentinel plus a single RAF-scheduled `scrollIntoView`, so live token streaming now keeps following correctly instead of only snapping to the end when the checkbox is toggled.
• 🐞 Preserved the transcript anchor across full rerenders and placeholder states, which fixes the broken follow behavior caused by rebuilding the transcript DOM while generation was still in progress.
• 🧪 Upgraded the browser smoke to measure real bottom-follow deltas during a mocked live stream, then revalidated the regenerated `web-ui.test.html` DOM with Playwright before shipping the new build.

v0.8.26

• ⚡ Reworked chat streaming follow mode to use a CSS bottom-anchor instead of repeated transcript `scrollTop` writes, which keeps token generation smooth while auto-scroll is enabled and still lets the checkbox disable follow mode instantly.
• 🐞 Replaced the composer’s JS height-sync loop with CSS-native textarea growth, removing the resize reflow churn that was making the transcript and input area stutter together during active chatting.
• 🧪 Added smoke coverage for the new transcript anchor/class toggle path, then rebuilt and reran the guarded build + smoke flow for the release.

v0.8.25

• 🐞 Reworked the transcript resize-handle gesture again so fit-to-content now uses a tolerant pointer-up triple-tap counter plus a no-pointer-events touch fallback, making the handle reliable on both desktop and mobile instead of depending on the browser’s fragile click-detail behavior.
• 🖥️ Nudged the chat transcript auto-scroll checkbox down another 3px to match the requested alignment under the transcript.
• 🧰 Expanded web/code_syntax.json further so every family now carries explicit operator/separator and related category fields in the file itself, and updated the renderer/build guardrails so those per-family entries are the validated source of truth instead of invisible shared fallback assumptions.

v0.8.24

• 🐞 Fixed the chat transcript resize handle triple-click path by moving fit-to-content detection off the fragile click event and onto a pointer-up tap counter that still works after pointer capture and tiny drag-handle interactions, including in the rebuilt test HTML artifact.
• 🧰 Normalized web/code_syntax.json so every language family now explicitly includes the full category/schema surface instead of sparse per-family fragments, making the supported token coverage visible and auditable directly in the file.
• 🧪 Hardened the build guardrails so the code-syntax source-sync test now fails if any family drops one of the required category fields, then rebuilt and reran the guarded build/smoke flow for the new release.

v0.8.23

• ⚡ Smoothed chat transcript streaming while auto-scroll is enabled by stopping programmatic bottom-follow scrolls from re-triggering the interaction defer path, so replies keep rendering token-by-token instead of bunching into visible batches once the transcript starts moving.
• 🖥️ Added a global chat transcript auto-scroll checkbox under the transcript with the same look as the log viewer toggle, kept it in the existing gap, and made the setting persist across conversations and reloads.
• 🐞 Removed the chat transcript resize cap, added triple-click fit-to-content on the resize handle, and expanded the code syntax theme/token coverage so dedicated categories like operators, classes, namespaces, labels, selectors, decorators, and other family-specific tokens use their own configured colors instead of falling back to shared ones.
• 🧪 Extended the smoke coverage for the new chat auto-scroll control and the dedicated operator-theme token path, then reran the local smoke harness before cutting the release build.

v0.8.22

• 🐞 Fixed the standalone web-ui.test.html artifact reporting a stale script version by wiring its generator to the current build metadata instead of the old imported fallback constant.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the retained test HTML artifact and reran the full guarded build/smoke suite so the version shown in the lab matches the shipped release.

v0.8.21

• ⚡ Smoothed live chat streaming further by locking the transcript viewport height during active generation and batching bottom-follow scroll work, which cuts the resize/reflow churn that was making long replies stutter once the transcript kept growing.
• 🐞 Removed the old code-syntax fallback path so the admin panel now uses the rewritten local code_syntax.json file as its only source of truth, with the embedded payload always overwriting that file on startup and the hardcoded per-block CSS token colors removed so live theme values can actually win.
• 🐞 Reworked generation-stat latency to represent request-to-prompt-start timing instead of whole-turn generation time, using TTFT plus prompt-processing throughput when available and only falling back to backend stream-open timing when the runtime gives nothing better.
• 🧪 Rebuilt coverage around the stricter code-syntax source path and the updated streaming behavior before cutting the new installer build.

v0.8.20

• ⚡ Reworked live chat transcript streaming so open Markdown now stays on a lightweight plain-text lane while a reply is still generating, then the finalized content snaps back through the full Markdown renderer once blocks or the whole reply close for smoother token-by-token updates.
• 🐞 Fixed live code-syntax refresh more aggressively by disabling admin-page/browser reuse, hard-busting /admin/code-syntax fetches with the running script version, and rehighlighting existing transcript code blocks from preserved raw source whenever colors, aliases, or keyword rules change.
• 🐞 Corrected generation stats latency reporting so live turns no longer publish a fake ever-increasing latency mid-stream; TTFT stays live during generation and the real end-to-end latency now appears only once the request actually finishes.
• 🧪 Added smoke coverage for lightweight streamed-markdown rendering, finalized Markdown formatting, forced retroactive syntax repainting, and the streaming-vs-final latency metric split before rebuilding the shipped installer.

v0.8.19

• ⚡ Reworked live chat transcript streaming so open Markdown now stays on a lightweight plain-text lane while a reply is still generating, then the finalized content snaps back through the full Markdown renderer once blocks or the whole reply close for smoother token-by-token updates.
• 🐞 Fixed live code-syntax refresh more aggressively by disabling admin-page/browser reuse, hard-busting `/admin/code-syntax` fetches with the running script version, and rehighlighting existing transcript code blocks from preserved raw source whenever colors, aliases, or keyword rules change.
• 🐞 Corrected generation stats latency reporting so live turns no longer publish a fake ever-increasing latency mid-stream; TTFT stays live during generation and the real end-to-end latency now appears only once the request actually finishes.
• 🧪 Added smoke coverage for lightweight streamed-markdown rendering, finalized Markdown formatting, forced retroactive syntax repainting, and the streaming-vs-final latency metric split before rebuilding the shipped installer.

v0.8.18

• ⚡ Isolated chat status polling further so background refreshes on the Chat tab now use a lightweight stats/action-state updater during live turns instead of running the broader chat UI renderer, reducing non-transcript DOM churn while generation is in progress.
• 🐞 Fixed the user-facing chat latency metric so it now freezes at first response / first token timing for that turn instead of climbing continuously like total wall-clock duration during generation.
• 🧪 Rebuilt and reran the local build + smoke suite after the chat refresh-path and latency fixes to keep the shipped installer aligned with the updated smooth-streaming behavior.

v0.8.17

• ⚡ Smoothed live chat generation again by cutting back mid-stream Markdown boundary rescans and deferring syntax-highlight work during active streaming unless a fenced code block actually finalizes, bringing realtime output behavior back closer to 0.8.12 / 0.8.13.
• 🐞 Fixed code-syntax updates end to end by serving /admin/code-syntax directly from the embedded payload when available, rewriting the sidecar file from that payload, and invalidating already-highlighted transcript code when the syntax config changes so updated colors and token rules repaint old chats too.
• 🖥️ Reworked the cache/context stats so KV now only shows real reported KV-cache usage from the runtime, while natural context fill is shown separately as context: used / max (%) instead of being mixed into the KV number.
• 🧪 Revalidated the updated streaming, syntax, and stats paths with a fresh release build and smoke pass after comparing the affected frontend behavior against the local 0.8.12 and 0.8.13 backups.

v0.8.16

• 🐞 Paused remote update checks while the Run Update modal is open, so the green update header and button state stay quiet until the modal closes and polling resumes.
• 🐞 Fixed live chat stream recovery so same-page generations no longer rebuild mid-turn from the temp stream cache, while refresh recovery still restores the in-progress assistant reply and refuses shorter stale cache snapshots.
• 🐞 Fixed code-syntax refresh so shipped updates now rewrite stale on-disk code_syntax.json, the browser prefers the live /admin/code-syntax payload over embedded JS fallback data, and removed theme tokens now clear their old CSS variables immediately.
• 🖥️ Cleaned up chat/runtime stat labels by removing the ~ prefix from per-message input counts and applying : separators consistently to per-message output/TTFT/tk-s labels plus generation-stat fields that were still using bare words.
• 🧪 Extended smoke coverage to verify syntax-theme variable cleanup for removed tokens and the updated colon-based chat message meta formatting.

v0.8.15

• 🐞 Paused remote update checks while the Run Update modal is open, so the green update header and button state stay quiet until the modal closes and polling resumes.
• 🐞 Fixed live chat stream recovery so same-page generations no longer rebuild mid-turn from the temp stream cache, while refresh recovery still restores the in-progress assistant reply and refuses shorter stale cache snapshots.
• 🐞 Fixed code-syntax refresh so shipped updates now rewrite stale on-disk `code_syntax.json`, the browser prefers the live `/admin/code-syntax` payload over embedded JS fallback data, and removed theme tokens now clear their old CSS variables immediately.
• 🖥️ Cleaned up chat/runtime stat labels by removing the `~` prefix from per-message input counts and applying `:` separators consistently to per-message output/TTFT/tk-s labels plus generation-stat fields that were still using bare words.
• 🧪 Extended smoke coverage to verify syntax-theme variable cleanup for removed tokens and the updated colon-based chat message meta formatting.

v0.8.14

• 🐞 Fixed chat-message persistence so per-message token/speed stats, conversation totals, generation flags, and other runtime metadata now survive reloads instead of being stripped out when the conversation is re-saved on the backend.
• ⚡ Reworked live chat streaming recovery so the browser now keeps saving partial assistant output during generation, the backend tracks in-flight stream state by conversation, and refreshing mid-turn can resume the live reply instead of dropping the in-progress output.
• 🐞 Corrected chat telemetry/UI reporting so short user prompts no longer show whole-request context totals as if they were per-message token counts, while the runtime stats card now receives incremental streaming metrics including live KV usage updates.
• 🖥️ Reduced mobile transcript freeze pressure by deferring heavy transcript DOM re-renders while the user is actively scrolling or resizing during generation, then catching back up once interaction settles.
• 🧪 Revalidated the edited Python and JavaScript sources with local syntax checks before cutting the release build.

v0.8.13

• 🐞 Fixed the remaining markup/Markdown rendering regressions: HTML/XML fenced blocks now use visible tag colors again, footnote and relative-anchor links scroll to their targets inside the chat transcript, Mermaid export buttons use new inline SVG icons, and admonitions now get type-matched accent colors plus 16x16 icons.
• 🐞 Corrected chat/runtime telemetry handling so per-message token badges no longer reuse cumulative request totals, conversation stats now report cumulative usage, inactive conversations no longer stay stuck on a generating status, and KV/prefix reporting now avoids stale peak carryover from prior requests.
• ⚡ Reworked long-running command/log streaming paths so model-install jobs now flush carriage-return progress updates into Audit Logs instead of stalling after a couple of visible lines, and the Debug Log viewer now includes an interactive bash command lane with Ctrl+Enter send support.
• 🖥️ Replaced model-family card summaries with generated family-level descriptions so each model card describes its available preset mix instead of repeating the first preset's `best_for` line.
• 🧪 Revalidated the edited Python and JavaScript sources with local syntax checks before cutting the release build.

v0.8.12

• 🐞 Restored visible full-markup syntax coloring for HTML/XML-style code blocks by wiring `.chat-syntax-tag` and `.chat-syntax-attribute` to their dedicated theme tokens instead of the generic variable/constant fallbacks that left most tags looking like plain text.
• 🖥️ Tuned the existing chat syntax palette so markup tags now use the same cyan family already present elsewhere in the UI, while attributes continue to use the existing green accent for clearer tag/attribute separation without introducing a new color scheme.
• 🧪 Extended the syntax smoke coverage so markup probes still verify real literal `<` / `>` rendering and now also assert that the active theme gives HTML tags and attributes distinct non-foreground syntax colors.

v0.8.11

• 🐞 Fixed HTML fenced-code rendering so markup blocks now display literal `<` and `>` characters again instead of leaking double-escaped `<` / `>` entities into the chat transcript.
• 🛠️ Reworked the markup syntax highlighter to tokenize raw HTML/XML input before the final safe-escape pass, which closes the double-escaping hole at the renderer layer instead of relying on fragile per-block workarounds.
• 🧪 Added smoke coverage for a full HTML document code block, including direct assertions that rendered transcript blocks and standalone syntax-highlight probes preserve literal angle brackets without `&lt;` / `&gt;` regressions.

v0.8.10

• 🐞 Fixed the runtime-inventory status path so compose variants whose canonical name/path is explicitly experimental, including `dual/eagle3-experimental`, now stay classified as Experimental all the way from backend inventory generation through the preset UI grouping.
• 🐞 Expanded the lightweight self-contained LaTeX renderer with a broader KaTeX-aligned baseline for common symbols and syntax, including the basic hbar/hslash/ell/imath/jmath/Re/Im/R/N/Z/Complex letter commands, the dots/cdots/ldots/vdots/ddots/mathellipsis family, `smallmatrix` and `subarray`, and `cr` row breaks.
• 🧪 Built and live-validated the preset fix against the remote admin DOM, then cut the proper `v0.8.10` release build and reran the full local smoke suite.

v0.8.9

• 🛠️ Removed the `--allow-stale-metadata` bypass from the live build pipeline, so `build.py` now always enforces the metadata freshness guard instead of allowing stale release rebuilds.
• 🐞 Fixed the Setup Assistant survey selection repaint, renamed it to Preset Recommendation Survey, kept explicitly experimental presets like `dual/eagle3-experimental` in the Experimental group, and aligned the Custom Model modal’s right column with a clearer sidebar header.
• 🧪 Cut a proper `v0.8.9` release build with fresh metadata and reran the full local smoke suite through the normal guarded build path.

v0.8.8

• 🐞 Stopped the installer and migration flows from mutating the upstream `club-3090` checkout before calling `scripts/update.sh`, fixing the dirty-working-tree failure reported when a fresh install hit the final upstream update step.
• 🛠️ Removed several genuinely unreferenced helper paths from the active split sources, trimming dead build, updater, runtime-inventory, shared, and instance-management cruft without changing live behavior.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the release artifacts and reran the full local smoke suite after the non-mutating installer change and dead-code cleanup so the shipped `v0.8.8` output matches the corrected source tree.

v0.8.7

• 🐞 Fixed the split-source web build mismatch so the real `web/` source now carries the Summary-tab, preview-grouping, advanced-preset, blocked-launch warning, and Setup Assistant fixes instead of only patching the generated bundle.
• 🐞 Rebuilt the shipped admin assets from the corrected split sources and revalidated the generated installer path, so this release actually packages the intended preset/UI fixes alongside the earlier runtime/download backend changes.
• 🛠️ Updated `build.py` cleanup behavior so successful builds remove generated root bundle files by default, with a new `--keep-files` escape hatch when you intentionally want to inspect the composed artifacts.
• 🧪 Ran a proper non-stale release build after bumping metadata, then reran the full smoke suite to produce the real `v0.8.7` installer output.

v0.8.6

• 🐞 Parallelized Global single/dual preset launches so the controller now fans out Docker boots across every eligible GPU or pair at once instead of waiting for each container to reach ready before starting the next one.
• 🐞 Reworked model-download tracking into a real multi-job flow, which allows multiple model installs to run concurrently, keeps each job visible in status, and stops one new download request from freezing the others' Audit Log progress.
• 🐞 Added a local Hugging Face fallback chain for Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B AutoRound downloads, fixing the stale upstream repo slug that was producing the `Qwen/Qwen3-MoE-A3B-Instruct-AutoRound-Int4-mixed` not-found failure on the live remote host.
• 🖥️ Fixed the Model Summary selector regression, regrouped NVLink + multi-GPU presets under Advanced Docker Presets, corrected preview/experimental/badge handling, added the blocked-launch hardware warning modal, and refreshed the Setup Assistant wording/logic including the gated Hugging Face custom-import lane.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the release artifacts and reran the full local smoke suite after the runtime, inventory, and preset-UI fixes so the shipped `v0.8.6` output matches the corrected behavior.

v0.8.5

• 🐞 Fixed the self-update completion path so the updater now reads the freshly written server/service state before running readiness waits, instead of trusting stale startup env from the long-lived updater daemon.
• 🐞 Stopped controller-only updates from waiting on a removed or disabled Caddy service when HTTPS is off, which was the real source of the multi-minute post-install stall seen on the live host after the `v0.8.x` migration.
• 🐞 Hardened `--update` / `--migrate` mode-state restoration so the installer now preserves prior `--online use-https` behavior from the deployed systemd/Caddy topology as well as `server_config.json`, preventing a bad persisted flag from silently downgrading the machine back to plain HTTP.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the release artifacts and reran the full local smoke suite after the updater-wait and HTTPS-state fixes so the shipped `v0.8.5` output matches the corrected behavior.

v0.8.4

• 🐞 Fixed the self-update completion path so the updater now reads the freshly written server/service state before running readiness waits, instead of trusting stale startup env from the long-lived updater daemon.
• 🐞 Stopped controller-only updates from waiting on a removed/disabled Caddy service when HTTPS is off, which was the real source of the multi-minute post-install stall seen on the live host after the `v0.8.x` migration.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the release artifacts and reran the full local smoke suite after the updater-wait fix so the shipped `v0.8.4` output matches the corrected post-update behavior.

v0.8.3

• 🐞 Fixed the remote update version resolver so the panel now fetches `build/metadata.json` from the exact same commit SHA as the installer script, instead of mixing a SHA-pinned installer lookup with a branch-based metadata URL that could lag behind on GitHub raw caches.
• 🛠️ Reworked the installer's self-update environment contract to ship a SHA-aware metadata URL template alongside the installer URL template, keeping the control and updater services aligned on one immutable raw-source scheme.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the full release artifacts and reran the complete local smoke suite after the resolver change so the shipped `v0.8.3` output matches the fixed immediate-update behavior.

v0.8.2

• 🛠️ Removed the old accumulated release-history shell constant from the shipped installer contract entirely, so `build.py` no longer injects major-release notes into the installer and the build validator now fails if that embed path comes back.
• 🧰 Switched the controller's remote update metadata path to use GitHub `build/metadata.json` as the sole source of changelog/release data, instead of re-parsing release history out of the remote installer script.
• 🖥️ Updated the Run Update modal to render both changelog sections strictly from the fetched GitHub metadata payload, eliminating the old local shell-metadata fallback for major-release notes.
• 🧪 Rebuilt the release artifacts, reran the full build/smoke suite, and verified the generated installer no longer contains the old release-history shell block at all.

v0.8.1

• 🖥️ Rebuilt the Setup Assistant into a real recommendation quiz that uses the upstream workload metadata already present in the v0.8 inventory, then maps the answers back into model-first preset suggestions plus a suggested custom-import reference profile.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Custom Model entry point with the new circular plus icon treatment, replaced the cramped import modal with a cleaner two-column layout, and added clearer safety/override guidance alongside detected-rig context.
• 📝 Corrected `REMOTE.md` to match the live server's actual internal admin listener target (`127.0.0.1:18008`) so future remote audits and tunnels hit the right backend hop.
• 🧪 Re-ran the local smoke suite and traced the live migration history far enough to confirm the 2026-05-20 cutover was an `--update` followed by repeated `scripts/setup.sh qwen3.6-27b` reruns, while still finding no same-day `/opt/ai/club-3090` backup created on 2026-05-20 or 2026-05-21.

v0.8.0

• 🧩 Migrated the preset inventory to the upstream v0.8 registry/profile model so the panel now discovers the full modern catalog, including the new Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B and Gemma 4 26B-A4B families, shared-compose registry aliases, and the richer install metadata those variants need.
• 🟢 Added a first-class Custom Model flow in Presets: the selector now includes a `[+] Custom Model` entry point, imports stream the upstream `pull.sh` output into Audit Logs, successful imports register persistent custom runtime cards/tabs, and custom model removal now tears down the registered compose/runtime state instead of leaving orphaned panel entries behind.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Setup Assistant around the newer upstream recommendation model, adding rig-aware guidance, clearer production/preview cues for the new curated families, and an explicit advanced path for upstream custom imports without blowing up the existing model-first UX.
• 🐞 Fixed multiple v0.8 launch-path regressions uncovered on the live host: custom runtimes now keep their own host-model install path when launching, the registry-backed rebuild path now classifies the newer AWQ/MoE variants correctly, and vLLM launches once again export the upstream nightly pin so updated compose files stop resolving to the broken `nightly-` image tag.
• 🛠️ Preserved the live host's sudo-safe Hugging Face CLI lookup improvement inside the newer upstream `setup.sh`, installed the missing `python-yaml` dependency required by the v0.8 profile loaders, and repaired the live admin white-page outage caused by accidentally deploying a split-source placeholder backend instead of the shipped embedded control bundle.
• 🧪 Rebuilt and reran the full local smoke suite repeatedly during the migration, validated the live HTTPS admin page renders again, confirmed the v0.8 inventory/status payload on the real host, exercised the live custom-model add/remove flow with a small public model, and restored the machine to the standard `vllm/dual` serving state afterward.

v0.7.12

• 🐞 Fixed the live Users tab save/delete flows so successful backend responses now update the visible directory cards immediately instead of leaving the panel stuck on stale `No API users configured yet` state until a later reload.
• 🐞 Fixed the custom API preset save/delete flows so the preset catalog now reconciles directly from the response payload, which removes the stale deleted-card behavior caught during the live audit.
• 🖥️ Corrected auxiliary-service status rendering so exited services like SearxNG no longer show contradictory `exited` and `stopped` states in the same card, and now surface exit-code detail consistently.
• 🛠️ Embedded `CHANGE_LOG_RELEASE` into the shipped installer metadata, sanitized control characters out of parsed changelog text, and fixed the remote update fetch helper's broken curl return path so status/update metadata handling is more robust.
• 🧪 Added smoke coverage for immediate Users/Presets payload reconciliation and exited-service status rendering, then rebuilt and reran the full release build with the updated metadata path.

v0.7.11

• ⚡ Restored incremental syntax highlighting timing for streamed replies so fenced code blocks now pick up coloring as soon as that block is finalized, instead of waiting for the entire assistant message to finish.
• 🧪 Reverified the full build and smoke suite after the syntax-highlight timing fix so the shipped `v0.7.11` artifact matches the corrected behavior.

v0.7.10

• ⚡ Fixed streaming markdown so inline single-backtick code like ``[1,2,3,4,5,6]`` renders correctly while a reply is still generating, and forced the final fenced-code syntax highlight pass as soon as generation ends instead of waiting for a tab switch.
• 🧪 Reverified the full build and smoke suite after the syntax-highlight timing fix so the shipped `v0.7.10` artifact matches the corrected behavior.

v0.7.9

• 🐞 Filtered `admin_auth_denied` noise out of the human audit stream while still preserving the structured event in `debug.log`, so repeated failed admin probes no longer spam the Audit viewer.
• 🐞 Stopped the large `----- admin result -----` / `----- admin error -----` payload dumps from polluting the Audit log pane and routed those verbose frontend diagnostics into the Debug log cache instead.
• ⚡ Fixed streaming markdown so inline single-backtick code like ``[1,2,3,4,5,6]`` renders correctly while a reply is still generating, and forced the final fenced-code syntax highlight pass as soon as generation ends instead of waiting for a tab switch.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Docker log instance selector for Global launches so it fans out into concrete targets like `GPU0 (Global)` and `PAIR0_1 (Global)` instead of collapsing back to a misleading single-scope default.
• 🧪 Added smoke coverage for the new audit filtering, debug-result routing, streaming inline-code rendering, and global Docker-log selector behavior, then reran the full build and smoke suite successfully.

v0.7.8

• 🛠️ Consolidated the retained v0.7 release history into a single `v0.7.0` section and removed all `v0.6.x` entries from `CHANGE_LOG_RELEASE`, shrinking installer metadata without losing the current major-series summary.
• ⚡ Packed `code_syntax.json` into the shipped control payload as gzip+base64, now unpacks it to `/opt/club3090-control/code_syntax.json` on startup, and serves it from `/admin/code-syntax` so the large syntax-config blob no longer rides inside shipped JavaScript.
• 🧰 Audited `control/chat.py`, removed an unused in-memory conversation index path, and kept the chat storage/runtime behavior unchanged while trimming a little more dead weight.

v0.7.0

• 🛠️ Removed the remaining inert legacy-dual backend stubs, legacy status payload field, dead log-branch special cases, and other no-op compatibility leftovers that no longer participated in live runtime behavior.
• ⚡ Deleted several unreferenced UI runtime formatter functions and collapsed more dead legacy branches so the shipped JavaScript and final installer payload shrink further without changing current scope behavior.
• 📝 Updated the design docs again to reflect the cleaner post-cleanup state and the narrower set of legacy-oriented naming that still remains for non-dual reasons.
• 📝 Rewrote the main design docs to describe the current split-source architecture, canonical scope model, persistence layout, and build pipeline more accurately.
• 📝 Added a measured explanation for why the installer artifact did not shrink after the legacy dual-runtime refactor, including concrete byte deltas and the remaining compatibility residue.
• 📝 Reworked the v0.8 migration model and plan around actual readiness: schema-first normalization, upstream pull/generate wrappers, generated compose handling, and a separate advanced path for imported/custom models.
• 🐞 Fixed the updater readiness probe so successful self-updates no longer log a false admin-listener timeout before completion.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Instances scope strip so Global comes first, GPU tabs stay grouped, built-in and custom pair tabs follow together, and Create Custom Pair now lives on the same row with proper separators.
• 🖥️ Replaced the remaining outlined modal close buttons with the larger plain close style, added a dedicated GPU picker for custom pairs, and upgraded Wake-on-LAN into a validated single-line modal that preloads the configured default MAC.
• ⚡ Stopped syntax highlighting from re-touching live streaming transcript messages, which removes the code-block flicker during generation and preserves highlighting once the reply finishes.
• 🐞 Removed the leftover two-GPU Global summary special case and fixed stray edit/delete icon encoding regressions found during the post-refactor audit.
• ⚙️ Reordered the default dual-selector preference so automatic pair rows now favor the live-validated vllm/dual-dflash recipe before falling back to heavier Qwen dual compose variants.
• 🧪 Confirmed on the live host that the built-in PAIR0_1 can boot on vllm/dual-dflash, answer /v1/models, and then hand the machine back to the original single-GPU runtime state afterward.
• 🐞 Fixed dual-instance compose launches so instance project directories now anchor on the compose file's own folder, which restores relative dual-patch mounts like ../../patches/vllm-marlin-pad/... and prevents PAIR0_1 launches from dropping the required /vllm/ path segment.
• 🧪 Re-ran the live pair-runtime exercise after the stop-path fix and used the failure details to confirm and correct the compose-directory resolution bug before cutting the final candidate.
• 🐞 Fixed stop_instance() so dual and single overlap handoffs no longer run the launch-time GPU memory guard during docker compose down, which allows busy runtimes to stop cleanly before a replacement scope starts.
• 🧪 Revalidated the live controller updater after the v0.7.1 fixes, confirming that staged-feed self-updates now refresh /opt/club3090-control/install-club3090-server.sh to the downloaded version and reset the updater service back to the canonical GitHub source afterward.
• 🐞 Fixed controller self-updates so the updater now downloads the remote installer into a temporary file, refreshes /opt/club3090-control/install-club3090-server.sh, and then executes that cached copy, which keeps the local installer cache aligned with streamed update runs.
• 🔄 Hardened the installer's self-update source normalization so the repo URL, ref, branch, and raw script template always snap back to the canonical GitHub feed instead of persisting temporary override values from a previous update environment.
• 🧪 Verified the corrected updater path against the live server by driving the real controller update flow from a staged test feed, waiting through service recovery, and confirming the updated runtime/status surface came back healthy on the host.
• 🧰 Split the backend monolith into the new control/ source tree, renamed the old telemetry slice to system.py, rebuilt the canonical target model around GLOBAL, GPU<n>, and PAIRx_y, removed the legacy dual-runtime behavior path, and made sequential auto-pairs the built-in dual layout while preserving custom pair groups.
• 🖥️ Split the admin frontend into the new web/ source tree, removed the GPU pairing toggle, updated scope and pair handling around automatic sequential pairs, and blocked deletion of built-in auto-pairs while leaving custom pair management intact.
• 🛠️ Reworked the build pipeline around build/, web/, and control/, extracted the smoke harness into build/smoke_tests.py, added the root build.py wrapper, moved build logs into logs/, and regenerated the root monolith artifacts from the split sources.
• 🧪 Refreshed the status smoke fixtures to the canonical auto-pair model and reran the full local build, syntax, minification, UI, HTML, API, chat, and log smoke suite successfully against the new layout.
• 📝 Added v07_PROGRESS_REPORT.MD and v07_CHECKLIST.MD so the v0.7 refactor track can be resumed cleanly mid-stream if the work is ever interrupted.
